name.markdownDescription:
Logical name for the query

Specifies a logical name associated with the query (e.g., `errorsQuery`) This value identifies this query operation in aggregated metrics reports. If not specified, defaults to a concatenation of [queryTarget](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#querytarget) and [querySearch](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#querysearchs)

readPrintProgress.markdownDescription:
Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console

Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console This value is commonly used when testing an integration to a remote endpoint. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

logLevels.markdownDescription:
Log levels to send to CloudWatch Logs for query progress tracking

Specifies which query event log levels are sent to CloudWatch Logs for tracking query progress. Valid values: `ERROR`, `INFO`, `DEBUG`, `PERF`.  If not specified, defaults to `ERROR`, `INFO`, `PERF`.  **Note:** `PERF` is required for console progress tracking (gauges, completion detection). Disabling it will cause the console to not detect query completion.  ``` yaml queryLogLevels: ERROR,INFO,PERF ```

logGroup.markdownDescription:
CloudWatch Logs log group for query event logging

Specifies the CloudWatch Logs log group name for logging query lifecycle events. The log group must be pre-created (e.g. by Terraform). If not specified or empty, query event logging is disabled.  ``` yaml queryLogGroup: /log10x/query ```

target.markdownDescription:
Logical prefix to filter index objects by

Specifies a logical name associated with the search target. This value identifies the app that produced the queried events (e.g., `acme-client`).  This value corresponds with the [indexWriteTarget](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexwritetarget) argument.

search.markdownDescription:
List of terms to search for

Specifies a search expression applied to the underlying storage via [TenXTemplate filters](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#tenxtemplate-filters) for efficient pre-filtering. Supports only basic operations: `&&` (AND), `||` (OR), == (equality), and [includes](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXString.includes) (substring match).  Example:   ``` js (level==INFO || level==WARN) && includes(text, "POST") ```  This narrows down data fetched from storage before in-memory processing.  To perform more complex in-memory filtering, specify [queryFilters](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#queryfilters).

filters.markdownDescription:
List of terms to search for

Specifies JavaScript expressions applied in-memory to all TenXObjects that match the [querySearch](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#querysearch) expression. Supports all valid [TenX JavaScript](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/) expressions for complex filtering, enrichment, and processing.  This provides precise, exact filtering after data is fetched from storage.  ``` yaml queryFilters:   - LevelTemplate.level == "ERROR"    - Math.max(this.price, 100) ```

from.markdownDescription:
Beginning of the search time range (inclusive)

Defines the beginning of the search time range (inclusive) expressed as a UNIX millisecond epoch value. This value commonly uses the [now](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXDate.now) function.  For example, to express the value of one hour before the current millisecond epoch, specify:  ``` yaml queryFrom: now("-1h") ```

to.markdownDescription:
End of the search time range (exclusive)

Defines the end of the search time range expressed as a UNIX millisecond epoch value (exclusive). This value commonly uses the [now](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXDate.now) function.  For example, to express the value of the current millisecond epoch, specify:  ``` yaml queryTo: now() ```

objectStorageName.markdownDescription:
Object storage logical name

Identifies the [Object Storage](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/#objectstorageclassname) against which this query is made (e.g., `AWS`).

indexContainer.markdownDescription:
Object storage container containing index objects

Specifies the object storage container (e.g., AWS S3 bucket) name in which to store [TenXTemplate Filters](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#tenxtemplate-filters)'. This value corresponds with the [indexWriteContainer](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index)

readContainer.markdownDescription:
Location of the output file for writing TenXObject field values

Specifies the object storage container (e.g., S3 bucket) name where input objects (e.g., log file) to scan are stored. This value corresponds with the [indexWriteContainer](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index)

objectStorageArgs.markdownDescription:
Custom object storage args

Custom arguments passed as a map to the constructor of the underlying [object storage](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#indexobjectstoragename). This list is expected to hold pairs of key values (e.g., args: [key1, value1, key2, value2])

processingTime.markdownDescription:
Max milliseconds alloted for this query

Milliseconds allotted for this query to complete. Once this period has elapsed the query will terminate regardless of whether [queryLimitResultSize](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#querylimitresultsize) has not been exceeded.

resultSize.markdownDescription:
Max byte volume of matching events to fetch

Volume in bytes of events matching the app prefix, terms and time range to fetch. Once this volume has been reached the query will terminate.  If [queryLimitProcessingTime](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#querylimitprocessingtime) has elapsed before the requested result volume has been fetched, the query will still terminate.

timeslice.markdownDescription:
Max time range to process by a scan worker instance

Specifies the max time duration (e.g., 1min) an instance of the [scan](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#scan) worker will traverse within the index. For example, if a query specifies a time range of `15min` and this value is set to `1m`, 15 instances of the scan worker will execute in parallel to scan through the index. If this value is 0, a single worker instance will sequentially scan through the index.

maxInstances.markdownDescription:
Max number of instances of the scan worker

Specifies the maximum number of [scan](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#scan) worker instances that will traverse the index. if the provided time range and [timeSlice](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#queryscanfunctionparalleltimeslice) provided will result in more instances than the provided value, the time slice will increase its size until the amount of workers needed will be less than this value.  For example, if a query specifies a time range of `60min` and a time slice of `1m`, while this value is set to `25`, the time slice will be effectively increased to `3m` to accommodate this.  The maximum value for this option is: 10,000 (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 1000)

threads.markdownDescription:
Max number of threads allocated per each 'scan' worker instance

Sets the number of JVM threads to scan through a time slice of the index within a [scan](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#scan) worker instance. For example, if [timeSlice](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#queryscanfunctionparalleltimeslice) is `10min` and this value is set to 10, scan each 1m index time range using a dedicated thread. If set to 1 or 0, a single thread will scan through the entire index time range (e.g., 10min). (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

url.markdownDescription:
Scan worker endpoint URL

Specifies the endpoint used to invoke the [scan](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#scan) worker

limitResultSizeInterval.markdownDescription:
Interval by which to check whether 'queryLimitResultSize' is exceeded

Interval by which to check whether [queryLimitResultSize](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#querylimitresultsize) that is the query has returned >= the request result volume.

scanFlushInterval.markdownDescription:
Interval to wait before posting pending 'stream' worker requests

Specifies a maximum interval (e.g., `2sec`) when scanning an index time slice to wait to submit pending [stream](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#stream) worker requests. If not set, all stream worker requests are sent after the index scanning process for a target index time slice has finished.

objects.markdownDescription:
Max number of input object byte ranges to process in a single 'stream' instance

Defines the maximum number of Storage objects to process by a single instance of the [stream](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#stream) worker.  For example, if set to 50 and the [scan](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#scan) phase has identified 200 potentially matching input object byte ranges, four instances of the stream worker will split the workload in parallel. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

byteRange.markdownDescription:
Max input object bytes for a single fetch request

Defines the max byte range within an input object (e.g., log file) that can fetched via a single request. For example, if this value is `5Mb` and two matching 1Mb byte ranges spaced 2Mb apart are identified within a target object fetch both from the storage via a single operation. If there is a 2Mb gap, launch two instances of the [stream](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#stream) worker.

url.markdownDescription:
Stream worker endpoint URL

Specifies the endpoint used to invoke the [stream](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/#stream) worker

vars.markdownDescription:
Specifies a list of variable values to scan for

Specifies a list of variable values to scan for

templateHashes.markdownDescription:
Specifies a list of TenXTemplate hash values to scan for

Specifies a list of TenXTemplate hash values to scan for

id.markdownDescription:
Unique ID of host query

UUID identifying this query. This value is automatically assigned if not explicitly set.

elapseTime.markdownDescription:
Epoch after which processing times out

Epoch value which if exceeds the current system time will result in this request being cancelled (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)
